marks_distribution: Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar (20 Marks)
Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks)
Internal Assessment: Listening and Speaking (5 Marks)
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Discursive passage (400-450 words, 10 marks) + Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words, 10 marks)
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech; Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation exercises (10 marks)
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph or Formal Letter (100-120 words, 5 marks) + Story/Diary Entry or Analytical Paragraph (100-120 words, 5 marks)
Reference to Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry (5+5=10 marks)
Short Answer Questions: From Beehive/First Flight (4 out of 5, 3 marks each) and Moments/Footprints Without Feet (2-3 out of 3, 2-3 marks each)
Long Answer Questions: From Beehive/First Flight and Moments/Footprints Without Feet (1 out of 2, 6 marks each)
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary
Writing Skills and Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone, format, fluency, accuracy
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying themes, understanding messages
internal_assessment: Activities: Role Play, Skit, Dramatization, Art-integrated projects
Parameters: Interactive competence, Fluency, Pronunciation, Language (Grammar/Vocabulary)
practical_assessment: Oral activities including role play and dramatization
Listening to authentic audio texts and retelling contents
Participating in conversations and discussions on topical interest
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on Role Play, Skit, Dramatization
Projects must follow Circular no. Acad-33/2020 dated 14 May 2020
